name: Sentry
one_liner: Application error monitoring and performance tracing platform with a comprehensive REST API for issues, events, projects, releases, alerts, and crons.
access_notes: Free Developer plan lets you create a Sentry account and generate working User Auth Tokens (Personal Tokens) with scoped permissions immediately, no payment or approval. Org-level auth tokens and internal integration tokens are also self-serve.
api_notes: Web API following REST conventions at us.sentry.io (region-aware: us2.sentry.io, de.sentry.io). Covers organizations, teams, projects, issues, events, releases, dashboards, discover, alerts, monitors/crons, replays, profiling, SCIM, and integrations - well over 100 documented endpoints. API version 0; public endpoints stable, beta endpoints may change.
mcp_notes: Sentry (getsentry) ships an official MCP server: hosted remote server at mcp.sentry.dev with OAuth 2.0 auth, plus the getsentry/sentry-mcp repo and @sentry/mcp-server npm package. Exposes issue lookup, event search, and Seer AI root-cause analysis.
main_blocker: None significant - public REST API with self-serve free-tier credentials. Only nuance is region-specific base URLs.

evidence.claim: Sentry provides a web API following REST conventions covering account, data, monitoring and integration areas; API version 0 with region-specific domains.
Sentry supports auth tokens (user personal tokens and org/internal-integration tokens), OAuth2 authorization code with PKCE, and device authorization flow.
Sentry ships an official hosted MCP server at mcp.sentry.dev with OAuth 2.0 and source at getsentry/sentry-mcp.

open_questions: Exact rate limits and whether every endpoint category (e.g. profiling, replays) is fully accessible on the free Developer plan versus requiring paid usage volume.
